title: Inkd Access Manifest
description: Multi-wallet access control manifest for private Inkd projects. Stored on Arweave. The Arweave hash of this document is stored on-chain in projectAccessManifest.

algorithm.description: Content encrypted with AES-256-GCM. The AES key is wrapped with ECIES (secp256k1) per recipient.

contentKey.description: The AES-256-GCM content key, wrapped separately for each authorized wallet.

publicKey.description: ECIES public key used for encryption (compressed secp256k1 hex). Stored for re-encryption without online wallet.

encryptedKey.description: AES content key encrypted with this recipient ECIES public key. Hex or base64.

role.description: Access level. read = decrypt only. write = push new versions. owner = full control.

addedAt.description: Unix timestamp when this recipient was added

versions.description: Per-version encryption info. Each version may have its own AES key (supports key rotation).

arweaveHash.description: Arweave hash of the encrypted content

keyRotatedAt.description: If set, this version uses a rotated key different from contentKey

encryptedKey.description: Only present if key was rotated. Same structure as contentKey.recipients.

revokedAt.description: If set, this manifest is superseded. New manifest hash is in the Registry.

_notes: To add a recipient: owner decrypts contentKey with their key, re-encrypts for new wallet, uploads new manifest to Arweave, updates on-chain hash via setAccessManifest().
To revoke a recipient: generate new AES key, re-encrypt all content, create new manifest without the revoked wallet, update on-chain hash.
Key rotation is expensive (re-upload content). Revocation is therefore best done proactively or for high-security scenarios.
